design-preview-cleanup
items.summary: Remove design preview code after a design is selected
items.bodyPreview:
# Design Preview Cleanup

- Create design mockups **only when the user explicitly requests** them (not proactive design-first for every feature).
- For Tool Hub and workspace UI features, Design Template is only for **active** review work, shown **in the Design Template tab** (5-card picker + inline preview).
- After the user selects/locks a design (`Design: V1`…`V5` or Lock in tab), delete the related preview scaffolding, mock data, variant registry, preview-only styles, and archived decision UI from production code.
- Do not keep "locked", "approved", or historical design data in the app unless the user explicitly asks for a durable design archive.
- If no designs are under active review, the Design Template body should render only `No active designs`, while keeping the shared header/search shell used by Hub/System pages.
- Keep the chosen production implementation and tests; remove only review/preview code that is no longer needed.

items.name: env-shared-standard
items.summary: Workspace shared secrets — E:\Dev\.env.shared for all P00xx tools
items.bodyPreview:
# Shared environment (`E:\Dev\.env.shared`)

- **Secrets shared across tools** (e.g. `SUPABASE_MANAGEMENT_TOKEN`) belong in `E:\Dev\.env.shared`, not in per-tool `.env.local` (those get overwritten on workspace sync).
- Template: `E:\Dev\.env.shared.example` (commit-safe). Real file is gitignored via `E:\Dev\.gitignore`.
- **Load order** in Node/Vite: `process.env` → `E:\Dev\.env.shared` → tool `.env.local` (tool wins only if you intentionally override).
- Use `Tool/scripts/lib/read-env-file.cjs` (`readSharedEnv`, `workspaceDevRoot`) — do not duplicate parsers in each repo.

When adding a new P00xx tool that needs workspace PATs, wire `readSharedEnv()` in vite/scripts on first setup; do not ask the user each time.

items.name: github-push-browser
items.summary: Push GitHub using the Cursor browser GitHub account through gh auth
items.bodyPreview:
# Push GitHub — dùng tài khoản browser Cursor

## Mong muốn của user

Khi push Git/GitHub: **dùng tài khoản đã đăng nhập trên browser Cursor** (tab GitHub agent đang dùng), **không** yêu cầu đăng nhập lại ở terminal, Git GUI, hay browser hệ thống khác.

## Giới hạn kỹ thuật (ngắn gọn)

- `git push` chạy trong **shell**, không đọc cookie tab browser.
- Cần **một lần** ủy quyền CLI qua **cùng browser Cursor** (device OAuth — user đã login thì chỉ bấm **Authorize github**).
- Sau đó `gh` lưu token local → mọi `git push` sau **tự động**, không hỏi login.

## Quy trình agent (bắt buộc khi user yêu cầu push)

1. `gh auth status` — nếu **đã** login → `git push` (hoặc `pnpm run push`).
2. Nếu **chưa** login:
   - Chạy `gh auth login -h github.com -p https -w` (background).
   - Lấy mã device từ output → `browser_navigate` https://github.com/login/device → nhập mã → **Authorize github** (click tọa độ nếu nút disabled trong snapshot).
   - `gh auth setup-git` (một lần).
3. `git push` — **không** hướng dẫn user `gh auth login` trên terminal riêng hay Credential Manager trừ khi bước 2 thất bại.

## Không làm

- Không nói "bạn cần login GitHub" khi tab browser đã là `tuanhoangfx` — hãy **chủ độ

items.name: p0004-design-template
items.summary: P0004 Design Template — on-demand mockups, in-tab compare, cleanup on lock
items.bodyPreview:
# P0004 Design Template

## When to create mockups

- **Only** when the user explicitly requests design review (5 variants, Design Template, "tạo mẫu", etc.).
- **Never** block production work or auto-scaffold previews for every new UI.

## Where mockups live

- **System → Design Template** tab only (`DesignTemplatePage.tsx`).
- Use the **5 variant cards** + **inline preview** pattern (sticky picker, full preview below).
- Prefer cloning existing Hub patterns (`modal-shell--tool-detail`, `overview-toc-nav`, detail tables).

## Active feature registry

- Register active reviews in `design-registry.ts`.
- Lock state: `lib/design-template-state.ts` (removes feature from tab until next request).
- One `*-mock.tsx` per feature with `V1`…`V5` exports.

## After user locks (`Design: V1` … `V5`)

1. Remove `*-mock.tsx`, preview-only styles, and registry entry for that feature.
2. Implement or confirm production UI in the real screen (e.g. Users modal).
3. Design tab shows `DesignTemplateEmpty` — **No active designs**.
4. Do not keep locked/approved mock data in the repo unless the user asks for an archive.

## Tool UI language

- All strings in Design Template and Tool Hub UI: **English**.

items.name: p0004-filter-hub-contract
items.summary: P0004 filter bar, faceted counts, and badge-registry icon contract for Tool/* UI
items.bodyPreview:
# P0004 filter & Hub tab UI contract

Reference implementation: `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub`.

## Filters

- Use shared `FilterBar` (`layout="hub"` on list/analytics screens). Tool UI strings in **English**.
- Every filter dimension: `FilterDef` + `showAllLabel: true` when “All {label}” applies.
- Faceted counts via `enrichFilterDefs` (or a thin wrapper); expose `totalCount` on def and `count` on each option.
- Icons only through `badge-registry.ts`:
  - `resolveFilterAllIcon(filterKey)` for trigger + “All …” row
  - `resolveFilterOptionIcon(filterKey, option)` for options
  - No `option.color` color-dots when registry has an icon
- New filter keys: add `FILTER_ALL` entry + `resolveFilterOptionIcon` cases before shipping.

## Hub / System / Users tabs

- Hub list: pinned filter chrome, `HubStickyHeader`, KPI/chart defs from hub prefs.
- System: `SystemTabSubNav` + per-tab `SystemTabHeader`; same filter bar pattern as Hub.
- Users: `UserDirectoryTable` + role badges via same registry as filters (`WORKSPACE_ROLE`).
- **User access modal** (`UserAccessModal`): same **Links panel** pattern as `ToolLinksPanel` — `FilterBar` + `tool-access-filters.ts` + `enrichFilterDefs` + `use-tool-access-fil

items.name: p0004-shell-ui-standard
items.summary: Use P0004 Tool Hub as the workspace UI shell standard
items.bodyPreview:
# P0004 Shell UI Standard

- **Canonical UI**: `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub` — shell, Hub/Users/System tabs, `FilterBar`, `badge-registry`, **Hub-like load** (local-first, SWR cache, eager routes, keep-mounted, shell-first), `HubLoadingView` (orb only when zero data), tokens (`p0008-globals.css`), compact 90% (`ui-scale.ts`).
- **Full migration workflow**: skill `.cursor/skills/sync-p0004-ui-shell/` + checklist `reference.md`.
- **Filters** (when touching filter files): rule `p0004-filter-hub-contract.mdc` — `enrichFilterDefs`, icons via registry, no color dots.
- Sync **presentation only**; preserve target data, auth, APIs, routes, storage, schema, and business logic unless explicitly asked.
- Prefer adapt/copy shell assets at boundaries; avoid overwriting domain routes or sample Hub data.
- **New UI**: design-first — 5 variants in System/Design Template (`workspace-design-preview-gate`) unless design is locked.
- Before broad edits: short plan → scoped layers (theme → shell → tabs → **loading** → filters → tables) → target `tsc`/lint/build.

## Settings / DisplayPrefs (clone to other tools)

Canonical: **`packages/hub-ui`** (`HubDisplayPrefs`, `.hub-check-indicator`), app adapter `src/co

items.name: p0020-cookie-agent-context
items.summary: Persistent context for P0020 Cookie Auto browser-agent workflow
items.bodyPreview:
# P0020 Cookie Agent Context

- Project: `Tool/P0020-Data-Box`; extension: `Extension/E0001-cookie-bridge`.
- Supabase project ref: `yhnqwxejjkfgmjmiquhb`; URL: `https://yhnqwxejjkfgmjmiquhb.supabase.co`.
- Cookie Auto local URL: `http://127.0.0.1:5177/?screen=cookie`.
- Route under test: `.facebook.com` / note `Cookie Facebook · TM-4b5ab60a`.
- Browser agents use Supabase tables `cookie_bridge_agents` and `cookie_bridge_commands`.
- Known agent IDs in this workflow: `33101c8f` is the Facebook-login source; `5ad932dc` is the no-login target.
- Prefer background agent commands (`Sync source`, `Load vault`, `Check`, `Reload ext`) over mouse/keyboard automation in Chrome profiles.
- Do not store Supabase service role keys, DB passwords, Facebook cookies, or raw cookie values in repo files or rules. Use existing browser login/session where possible.
- When changing unpacked extension manifest/source, manual `chrome://extensions` reload may still be needed; `Reload ext` only reloads the extension runtime.

items.name: p0020-cookie-icon-standard
items.summary: Standardize Cookie Auto brand icons across P0020 Web and E0001 Extension
items.bodyPreview:
# P0020 Cookie Auto Icon Standard

- Khi chỉnh Cookie Auto routes, luôn ưu tiên brand icon trước fallback generic.
- Source ưu tiên: theSVG CDN (`https://thesvg.org/`) bằng slug brand. Nếu theSVG không có, kiểm tra favicon/logo từ website. Nếu URL ngoài bị chặn hoặc không ổn định, lưu asset local.
- Nguồn chuẩn duy nhất là `Tool/P0020-Data-Box/src/features/cookie/cookieBrandIcons.registry.json`.
- Sau khi sửa registry, chạy hoặc cập nhật output tương đương từ `Tool/P0020-Data-Box/scripts/sync-cookie-brand-icons.mjs` để Extension dùng `Extension/E0001-cookie-bridge/cookie-brand-icons.generated.js`.
- Không hardcode mapping riêng lẻ trong Web/Extension ngoài registry.
- Kalodata đang dùng asset local đã lưu:
  - Extension: `assets/brand-icons/kalodata.png`
  - Web: `public/assets/brand-icons/kalodata.png`
- Surfshark dùng theSVG slug `surfshark`.
- Khi thêm brand mới, cập nhật changelog/version metadata Extension nếu popup behavior/asset thay đổi.

items.name: user-rules-version-gate
items.summary: User Rule — bắt buộc bump version khi sửa product; không chờ user nhắc (mirror cho Cursor User Rules)
items.bodyPreview:
# Version gate (User Rule)

Khi sửa `Tool/P00xx-*` hoặc `Extension/E00xx-*`: trước khi kết thúc task **bắt buộc** bump version theo `e:\Dev\.cursor\rules\workspace-version-bump.mdc` và báo **Version `X.Y.Z` → `A.B.C`**. Không chờ user nhắc.

Đồng bộ: `package.json`, `tool.manifest.json` (`release.version`), `CHANGELOG.md`, và `app-release.ts` (P0021) nếu có.

items.name: workspace-version-bump
items.summary: 3-tier manual version bumps (edit/commit/push) + agent checklist + git tag on official push
items.bodyPreview:
# Workspace version — agent bump thủ công (không script)

## Cổng kết thúc task (bắt buộc — không chờ user nhắc)

Nếu trong session bạn đã **sửa code hoặc UI có nghĩa** trong bất kỳ `Tool/P00xx-*` hoặc `Extension/E00xx-*`:

1. **Trước** khi trả lời “xong” / trước commit / trước push: bump version đúng tầng và đồng bộ mọi file trong bảng dưới + `CHANGELOG.md`.
2. Trong tóm tắt cuối **luôn** ghi: `Version: X.Y.Z → A.B.C` (hoặc “không bump — chỉ đọc/whitespace”).
3. Product có rule riêng (vd. `P0021-AutoVideo-Studio/.cursor/rules/*-version-bump.mdc`): đọc và làm theo checklist file cụ thể.

**Không** kết thúc task chỉ vì user chưa nói “nhớ bump version”.

Format `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`. Mỗi **product** (một `Tool/P00xx-*` hoặc `Extension/E00xx-*`) giữ **một dãy version riêng**.

## 3 tầng (agent tự sửa file, không dùng script/hook bump)

| Tầng | Khi nào | Cách tăng | Ví dụ |
|------|---------|-----------|--------|
| **Patch** | Hoàn thành task có **đổi code/UI có ý nghĩa** | `PATCH + 1` | `0.1.12` → `0.1.13` |
| **Minor** | User yêu cầu **commit** (hoặc agent commit trong task) | `MINOR + 1`, `PATCH = 1` | `0.1.13` → `0.2.1` |
| **Major** | User yêu cầu **push GitHub chính thức** lên `ma

items.name: p00xx-release-workflow
items.trigger: Runs a standard release workflow for Tool/P00xx repositories: verify build/tests, commit safe changes, push GitHub, deploy to Vercel or VPS, smoke test production, and create the GitHub release. Use when the user asks to release, deploy, push, publish, create a GitHub release, or execute numbered suggestions that include build, commit, push, Vercel/VPS deploy, smoke test, or release for a P00xx tool.
items.summary: Runs a standard release workflow for Tool/P00xx repositories: verify build/tests, commit safe changes, push GitHub, deploy to Vercel or VPS, smoke test production, and create the GitHub release. Use when the user asks to release, deploy, pu
items.bodyPreview:
# P00xx Release Workflow

## Scope

Use this for `Tool/P00xx-*` repositories. It standardizes release work while respecting each repo's own scripts and deployment target.

## Safety Rules

- Only commit, push, deploy, or create releases when the user explicitly asks.
- Never commit secrets or local runtime data: `.env*`, credentials, Supabase service role keys, DB passwords, cookies, VPS env files, backups, private exports, or token-bearing config.
- Preserve unrelated user changes. If the working tree has unrelated edits, leave them out of the commit or ask when separation is impossible.
- Do not use destructive git commands, force-push, skip hooks, or change git config unless the user explicitly requests it.

## Workflow

1. Identify the repo:
   - Confirm current tool path, branch, remote, package version, and deployment target.
   - Read `package.json`, `tool.manifest.json` if present, `.vercel/project.json` if present, and relevant deploy docs/scripts.

2. Inspect work to release:
   - Run `git status --short --branch`, `git diff --stat`, `git diff`, and recent `git log`.
   - Decide which files are relevant and safe to stage.
   - Warn about secrets or unrelated files.

3. Ve

items.summary: Syncs the P0004 Tool Hub UI shell, design tokens, typography, spacing, effects, sidebar/header/tab patterns, Hub-like data loading (stale-while-revalidate, eager tabs, keep-mounted, build snapshots, background sync), HubLoadingView, FilterB
items.bodyPreview:
# Sync P0004 UI Shell

## Goal

Apply `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub` as the visual and structural standard for a target tool **without** changing domain behavior, data, or backends.

## When to use

- New P00xx fork or “làm giống Hub / P0004”
- Đồng bộ filter, tab header, font, sidebar, table style, **loading UI**
- Audit tool đích so với Hub (Users, System, Hub list)

## Architecture (what to copy vs share)

| Approach | When |
|----------|------|
| **Copy + adapt** (current) | One-off migration; fastest |
| **Extract `packages/hub-ui`** (future) | 3+ tools need identical shell; worth refactor later |
| **Rule + skill only** | Agent remembers contract; manual copy per screen |

Start with **copy + adapt** using the manifest in [reference.md](reference.md).

## Workflow

1. **Identify** source `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub`, target `Tool/P00xx-*`.
2. **Read** [reference.md](reference.md) manifest; open listed source files.
3. **Read target**: `package.json`, entry, theme, layout, existing filters/tabs.
4. **Plan** (short): shell → tokens → tabs → **Hub-like load** → filters → tables/cards → verify.
5. **Migrate** in order (see reference.md “Migration order”).
6. **Verify**: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`,

items.summary: Finds missing product identity icons, designs 5 review variants, and synchronizes approved SVG icons across P0004 Tool Hub, P00xx tools, E00xx extensions, manifests, favicons, and app/extension branding. Use when the user asks to scan icons
items.bodyPreview:
# Tool Icon Identity Workflow

## Scope

Use this skill for product identity icons for `Tool/P00xx-*` and `Extension/E00xx-*`.

Do not use it for generic UI icons, table glyphs, third-party brand icons, or route/status icons unless the user explicitly asks.

## Source Of Truth

- Tool Hub icon assets: `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub/public/icons/tools/<CODE>.svg`
- Tool Hub mapping: `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub/src/lib/visual.ts`
- Product manifests: `tool.manifest.json`
- Web app icon targets: app metadata, favicon, public icon assets
- Extension icon targets: `manifest.json` `icons` and `action.default_icon`, popup/header branding

## Required Workflow

1. Scan inventory:
   - Read `Tool/P*/tool.manifest.json` and `Extension/E*/tool.manifest.json`.
   - Extract `code`, `name`, `type`, `status`, and optional `icon`.
   - Compare with `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub/public/icons/tools/<CODE>.svg`.
   - Check whether the source repo also has its own app/extension icon path.

2. Report gaps before editing:
   - `missing-hub-icon`: no `<CODE>.svg` in P0004.
   - `missing-manifest-icon`: manifest has no `icon`.
   - `missing-source-icon`: source repo has no favicon/app/extension icon.
   - `needs-review`: icon exis

items.name: workspace-design-preview-gate
items.bodyPreview:
# Workspace design template gate

## When to use

- User **explicitly** asks for design review, 5 mockups, Design Template, or `Design: V1`…`V5`
- **Do not** scaffold design previews proactively for every new feature

## Rules

1. **On-demand only** — no design-first gate before production unless the user requests it.
2. **In-tab only** — mockups live under **System → Design Template** (`/system/template`), with the existing 5-card picker + inline preview (not separate `/design-preview/*` routes unless the user asks).
3. **≥5 distinct variants** per feature; static mock data; clone existing Hub shells when relevant (e.g. `modal-shell--tool-detail` + TOC).
4. **After lock** — when the user says `Design: V1`…`V5` (or uses Lock in the tab):
   - Delete `*-mock.tsx`, preview-only CSS, and registry entries for that feature
   - Update `design-registry.ts` / remove feature from active list
   - Tab shows **No active designs** until the next review is requested
   - Keep production implementation only; no durable “approved design archive” in app code unless asked
5. Document `docs/DESIGN-PREVIEW-{feature}.md` during review; optional `docs/DESIGN-DECISION.md` after lock.

## P0004 Tool Hub
